lines.text: Tucker in field, West Side perimeter near Monroe Street. It's {{CLOCK}} Madison. We're {{ELAPSED}} into the night and the analytics are telling an interesting story.

Madison-wide cumulative is sitting at 139 kills as of my last sync. That's 17 ahead of pace from 2168 at this hour — we were at 122 last year, same time. East Side still holds the majority, but West Side activity is accelerating. Nakoma corridor has seen a 22% uptick in the last ninety minutes.

I'm looking at the Hilldale area right now. Residential, mostly barricaded, but there's a lot of vehicle movement on the service roads. Secondary escapes, possibly — people routing around the main corridors. Holden, you seeing that in the traffic data?

The Beltline overpass is backing up. Vehicle-on-vehicle incidents are up 31% compared to this hour in 2168. If people are routing west to avoid the downtown congestion, that explains your Nakoma spike.

Acknowledged. Weapons methodology on the West Side is skewing toward bladed and environmental — fewer firearms, which tracks with the economic data we've been discussing. The neighborhood is more barricade-heavy, less firepower-dependent.

I'm repositioning north on Monroe. There's a structure fire approximately four blocks — visibility is low with the fog. I'm moving to get a better angle on the perimeter, it looks like the fire department volunteers are staging, so —

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide at 02:00: 139 cumulative. +17 vs. 2168 same hour. East Side 34% of total. West Side climbing.
Nakoma corridor +22% activity, last 90 minutes. Monroe Street residential barricade density high. Hilldale secondary escape routes active.
Structure fire, West Side (Monroe/Hilltop area). Volunteer response staged. Fog limiting visibility to approximately 1.5 miles.
PurgeShield structural reinforcement: West Side installations up 18% YoY. Hilldale area seeing record panel demand. Call 608-SHIELD-1.
